subsystems.name: Authentication & Authorization
subsystems.purpose: JWT token generation, verification, and role-based access control
responsibilities: User registration and login with bcrypt password hashing
criticalConstraints: All controller functions must be wrapped with asyncHandler
Role guards enforced at route level, not controller level
JWT tokens contain user ID and role only
Password hashing uses bcrypt with salt rounds = 10

subsystems.name: User Management
subsystems.purpose: User entity creation, profile management, and role assignment
responsibilities: Admin user listing and status toggling
criticalConstraints: Email must be unique across User collection
Role is immutable after user creation
Admin can toggle user active status but not delete

subsystems.name: Doctor Profiles & Verification
subsystems.purpose: Manage doctor professional information, verification status, credentials, and practice team hierarchies
responsibilities: Credentials and license document upload to Cloudinary
Doctor profile display in public discovery
Practice team and junior doctor invitation management
criticalConstraints: Only verified doctors appear in public discovery
consultationFee is snapshotted to Appointment at booking time
Doctor cannot have duplicate specializations
Suspended doctors cannot accept new bookings
ADR-003: Cloudinary resource_type 'raw' for credentials
Junior doctors inherit verification status but cannot set fees, manage availability, or update onboarding templates

subsystems.name: Patient Profiles
subsystems.purpose: Manage patient medical information, contact details, and profile customization
responsibilities: Patient profile creation on first login
Profile updates (name, phone, medical history, address)
criticalConstraints: One PatientProfile per User
Medical history is patient-controlled and never visible to doctor
Avatar is optional

subsystems.name: Availability Management
subsystems.purpose: Doctor availability scheduling with weekly recurring patterns and slot management
responsibilities: Mark slots as booked during appointment creation
Unlock slots if appointment creation fails
Provide available slot list for booking flow
Allow doctor to update or delete slots
criticalConstraints: ADR-001: Slot locking uses findOneAndUpdate with isBooked:false condition
Slots must be unlocked if Appointment creation fails after lock
Time format is HH:mm string, date is YYYY-MM-DD string
Slots cannot be created in the past
One WeeklySchedule per doctor per day-of-week

subsystems.name: Doctor Discovery & Search
subsystems.purpose: Find verified doctors through listing, geospatial proximity, full-text search, and AI recommendations
responsibilities: Full-text search by name, specialization, clinic name
criticalConstraints: Only verified doctors appear in results
Results include aggregated review ratings
Geospatial queries require 2dsphere index on coordinates
Search suggestions are real-time, not cached
Public endpoints (no auth required)

subsystems.name: Appointment Booking & Management
subsystems.purpose: Core booking flow with atomic slot locking, fee snapshotting, and appointment lifecycle
responsibilities: Fee snapshot from DoctorProfile at booking time
criticalConstraints: ADR-001: Use findOneAndUpdate with isBooked:false for atomic locking
ADR-004: Fee snapshot from DoctorProfile, never recalculate
ADR-006: platformCommission always 10%, doctorEarnings always 90%
Appointment cannot proceed without payment
Date format is YYYY-MM-DD string, time format is HH:mm string
Cancellation reason must be provided by cancelling user
ADR-002: Payment is separate collection, not embedded in Appointment

subsystems.name: Payment Processing (Razorpay)
subsystems.purpose: Razorpay integration for order creation, payment capture, and signature verification
responsibilities: Create Razorpay orders linked to appointments
Provide payment history to patients and admin
Prevent payment manipulation via signature verification
externalServices: Razorpay: order creation, payment capture, refunds
criticalConstraints: ADR-006: Amount must be converted to paise (multiply by 100) before Razorpay API
ADR-009: Signature must be verified before updating payment status
ADR-004: Fee snapshot must be read from Appointment, never recalculated
Payment status transitions: created → paid (or failed)
Razorpay signature verification uses HMAC-SHA256
Database stores amounts in rupees, Razorpay API expects paise

subsystems.name: Refund Management & Processing
subsystems.purpose: Request, approve, and process refunds with Razorpay integration
criticalConstraints: Refund rejection must include explanation in refundAdminNote
Doctor cancellation auto-refunds without admin approval
Patient cancellation requires admin approval
Refund status flow: none → requested → pending → approved/rejected → processed
Once processed, refund is irreversible
ADR-006: Razorpay API expects paise, database stores rupees

subsystems.name: Reviews & Ratings
subsystems.purpose: Patient review submission, visibility control, and review-based doctor discovery
responsibilities: Patient review submission for completed appointments
Public review display on doctor profiles
criticalConstraints: Reviews can only be submitted for completed appointments
One review per appointment (prevent duplicates)
Rating is 1-5 integer
Only visible reviews appear in public discovery
Admin can toggle review visibility
Review is marked isVerified=true when linked to completed appointment

subsystems.name: AI Integration & Recommendations
subsystems.purpose: OpenAI-powered symptom triage, doctor profile summaries, and clinical exercise generation
responsibilities: Free-text symptom interpretation and specialization recommendation
Batch summary generation for all verified doctors
Summary caching to reduce API calls
Structured clinical exercise generation from natural language prompts
externalServices: OpenAI: GPT-3.5 for text generation and clinical parameters modeling
criticalConstraints: Symptom interpretation is free (no auth required)
Doctor summaries are cached per doctor
Batch summary generation is admin-only
Summary cache is invalidated when doctor profile changes
AI responses must be deterministic (use temperature=0.3)
AI exercise generation is rate-limited to 10 requests per minute per doctor profile

subsystems.name: Admin Dashboard & Operations
subsystems.purpose: Platform-wide management: doctor verification, user management, analytics, and refunds
criticalConstraints: All admin endpoints require role: admin
Role guard enforced at route level
Analytics aggregations must not impact live transaction performance
Audit logs capture all admin actions
Doctor verification workflow is irreversible once approved/rejected

subsystems.name: Notifications & Email
subsystems.purpose: In-app and email notifications for appointments, payments, reviews, and admin actions
responsibilities: Provide notification history and unread count
Fire-and-forget email sending (never await in critical flows)
criticalConstraints: ADR-008: Never await email sending in critical flows
Email is sent asynchronously via job queue or background worker
In-app notifications are created synchronously
Notification type includes: booking, cancellation, payment, refund, review
Email must include appointment details and action links

subsystems.name: Document & Media Storage
subsystems.purpose: Cloudinary integration for credentials, session notes, avatars, and media files
externalServices: Cloudinary: file storage, transformation, and delivery
criticalConstraints: ADR-003: Use resource_type: 'raw' for PDF credentials
ADR-003: Use resource_type: 'auto' for images
All file uploads must validate file type and size
Cloudinary public_id stores file reference for deletion
Doctor cannot access patient media, and vice versa

subsystems.name: Waitlist Management
subsystems.purpose: Patient interest tracking for unavailable doctors with notification on availability
responsibilities: Patient join waitlist for unavailable doctor
criticalConstraints: One waitlist entry per patient-doctor pair
Duplicate join attempts should be idempotent
Status transitions: active → notified → completed/expired

subsystems.name: Session Records & Care Continuity
subsystems.purpose: Clinical documentation layer linking completed appointments to recovery plans
responsibilities: Create session record linked to completed appointment
Maintain progress rating, presenting condition, and pain score deltas
Prescribe and detail exercises (sets, reps, frequency, duration, notes)
Log recommended medications and supplemental clinical notes
Enable HIPAA-aligned visibility toggles (share with patient)
Provide patient-facing care timelines with paginated progress summaries
criticalConstraints: One SessionRecord per Appointment (enforced at DB level)
Records can only be edited within 24 hours of signature (measured from doctorSignedAt)
All edits within the 24-hour window append metadata changes to editHistory array
Archived records are soft-deleted (hidden from facing lists, kept for admin compliance review)
Timeline and history queries use parallelized aggregations
